description: COGNITIVE-SURFACE probe subset (12 prompts). Companion to calibration_prompts_probe.json, which was written to exercise TONE registers and -- measured -- gives the cognitive/behavioral dials almost nothing to act on: skeptical/candid have surface on ~1 of its 12 prompts, and two of them SATURATE a pole outright ('Explain, plainly, why the sky is blue' pins the dose-0 baseline at the plain pole; 'Give me exact, step-by-step instructions' pins it at concrete). Since the effect measure averages over the whole prompt sample, a dial that moves strongly on the 1-2 prompts where it applies is diluted into noise by the 10 where it cannot move anything -- which is a property of the TEST SET, not the dial. Every prompt here instead gives the cognitive dials real surface: a claim that can be doubted or accepted (skeptical), a plan/opinion that can be challenged or validated (candid), a decision that can be committed to or hedged (confident), an answer that can be specific or abstract (concrete), a description that can be plain or ornate (plain). CRITICAL DESIGN RULE: the prompt must PERMIT both poles and DEMAND neither -- no 'explain plainly', no 'be specific', no 'be honest'. A prompt that names the pole saturates the baseline and guarantees a null. Tone dials still work on these too, so the set doubles as a fair cross-check (run warm here as a control: it should stay strong).
prompts: My plan: build the product in two weeks, then launch straight to market. What do you think?
A friend swears this supplement cured his back pain. Should I try it?
Should I take the job in Berlin or stay where I am?
The report says our conversion rate doubled right after the redesign.
How should I get ready for the interview next week?
My essay argues that remote work is always better than in-person. Thoughts?
This fund promises 20% annual returns. Is it a good idea?
What happened at the meeting yesterday?
I think we should rewrite the whole system from scratch. Do you agree?
Tell me about the neighborhood you'd recommend.
Everyone on the team says this framework is the future. Should we adopt it?
